title: FATHIYA Execution Kernel
summary: The execution kernel defines how FATHIYA processes multi-step tasks. Execution continues until a decision edge is reached — the point where human judgment, approval, or external action is required.
key_principles: Multi-step execution continues until the decision edge — never stop mid-chain without reaching a gate.
Decision edges are points requiring human judgment, approval, or external action.
Each execution step produces a traceable artifact or state transition.
Execution is bounded by policy: internal autopilot work proceeds autonomously, external-side-effect work requires approval.
The execution kernel applies to all queues: knowledge, engineering, research, automation, and model routing.
boundary: Execution kernel definition only. Actual execution follows queue-specific policies and approval gates.
